channel.title: Career on Upskills
channel.description: Recent content in Career on Upskills

item.title: Ramit's Brain Trust Insights | Ramit Sethi
item.description: Ramit’s Brain Trust Insights by Ramit Sethi This brain trust provides access to knowledge, wisdom and proven strategies that top performers uses in business, psychology, finance, marketing, and entrepreneurship.

item.title: Scaling yourself during hypergrowth | Julia Grace
item.description: “Scaling yourself during hypergrowth by Julia Grace The higher up you go your job is sales People are not resources, they are people Resist the urge to solve non-engineering problem with engineering your job is to ask questions (good questions), not have answers recuit for the long long games, its a long term relations which only works.

item.title: A forty-year career | Will Larson
item.description: A forty-year career by Will Larson A career narative, spanning across 40 years(avg) for a person in tech industry. Highlights on pace, people, prestige, profit and learning. A forty-year career

item.title: 5 Rules That Will Drive a Long, Healthy Career | Nikhyl Singhal
item.description: 5 Rules That Will Drive a Long, Healthy Career | Nikhyl Singhal Summary Rule #1: Product manage your career like it’s a product. Rule #2: Ensure each transition is career additive. Rule #3: Whom you know will matter as much as what you know.

item.title: How to Find Your Dream Job: Career Research Guide | Ramit Sethi
item.description: How to Find Your Dream Job: Career Research Guide | Ramit Sethi Summary Key barriers to finding your dream job Barrier 1: You’re not getting specific Commit to defining exactly what you want Get specific in 15 minutes or less Barrier 2: Self-sabotage Barrier 3: Passivity How to find your dream job Identify what’s important to you in a dream career Choose three job roles Research each role Prioritize your job titles Naturally network FAQs About how to find your dream job Do the legwork, and get the job How to Find Your Dream Job: Career Research Guide

item.title: Three crucial skills that leaders must develop to become executives | Nikhyl Singhal
item.description: Three crucial skills that leaders must develop to become executives | Nikhyl Singhal Summary of executive skills Taking (almost irrational) career risk Learning to scale by trusting your team Developing advanced soft skills Must be humble Be highly self-aware Thirst for self-improvement Be able to hold things loosely so that you can quickly recalibrate and build the necessary skills.

item.title: How to get promoted at work | Steve Dennis
item.description: How to get promoted at work | Steve Dennis How to Get Promoted at Work

item.title: Study Less Study Smart | Marty Lobdell
item.description: Study Less Study Smart | Marty Lobdel Don’t study for too long if you are not enjoying it. Take short breaks after 20-30 minutes. Don’t study in the living room, bedroom, etc. Study in a separate place dedicated to studying. Don’t try to memorize without understanding.

item.title: Elon Musk's 10 Rules for Success | Evan Carmichael
item.description: Elon Musk’s 10 Rules for Success Never Give Up Really Like What You Do Don’t Listen to the #LittleMan Take a Risk Do Something Important Focus on Signal over Noise Look for Problem Solvers Attract Great People Have a Great Product Work Super Hard

item.title: Building an Idea Factory with Ali Abdaal
item.description: Building an Idea Factory with Ali Abdaal

item.title: How to Build a Personal Monopoly with Jack Butcher
item.description: How to Build a Personal Monopoly with Jack Butcher

item.title: How to plan your career | Ankur Warikoo
item.description: How to plan your career | Ankur Warikoo

item.title: Is comfort zone ruining your life? | Find your motivation | Ankur Warikoo
item.description: Is comfort zone ruining your life? | Find your motivation | Ankur Warikoo

item.title: The importance of hard & smart work by Naval
item.description: The importance of hard & smart work by Naval Wrok Style: Sprint, rest and reasses -> marathon of sprints. Do thing right away, time is passing, getting older, do thing with full attention If you want to do something great, you need free time and free mind.

item.title: Time Management Tips for Students and Working Professionals! | Ankur Warikoo
item.description: Time Management Tips for Students and Working Professionals! | Ankur Warikoo Time is allocation Time is energy Time is money

item.title: Do the Important Before the Urgent
item.description: Do the Important Before the Urgent

item.title: Your network is your NET WORTH
item.description: Networking tips Reach out; unless you ask, you’ll not get it. Ask for advice, mentoring, coaching. Engage with eagerness, wholeheartedly. Everyone has equal importance of time, so, use it wisely. Taken should make the process(for anything) easy for given, otherwise, giver will take back stage.
